frameworks.name: Bayesian optimal observer
frameworks.one_line: ideal computation of posterior probability of correct response under variable reliability

frameworks.name: evidence accumulation with collapsing/urgency bound
frameworks.one_line: linear urgency signal added to accumulated evidence to drive commitment

frameworks.name: predictive-coding-style precision weighting
frameworks.one_line: samples weighted by inverse prediction error to track changing reliability

frameworks.name: speed-accuracy trade-off in decision making
frameworks.one_line: balancing information harvesting against opportunity cost of deliberation

core_question: How do humans decide when to stop sampling and commit when evidence reliability itself varies unpredictably over time?
key_assumptions: observers can estimate the moment-to-moment reliability of evidence
deliberation incurs an opportunity cost that grows with time
decision commitment occurs when accumulated evidence crosses a threshold
manipulations: categorization of serially presented oriented gratings
variable variance (heteroscedasticity) of decision-relevant information across time
free choice of stopping time by the observer
key_findings: humans adaptively down-weight samples with high prediction error
accumulated evidence is combined with a linear urgency signal to a threshold
this heuristic model approximates the full Bayesian posterior of correctness
the model predicts adaptive weighting reflected in neural signals
interpretations: optional stopping under heteroscedasticity is approximated by precision-weighted accumulation plus urgency
an approximate algorithm can implement near-Bayesian optimality in non-stationary environments
